name: Dick's Sporting Goods
best_contact_role: Investor Relations / Corporate Communications (Nate Gilch, SVP Communications)
why_target: Largest US sporting goods retailer; will be the dominant national stockist of WC2026 Adidas match ball, USMNT replica jerseys and competing-nation kits across all 50 states; host-nation surge means football moves from niche to mainstream in their footprint
notes: HQ: 345 Court Street, Coraopolis, PA 15108. Parent: Dick's Sporting Goods Inc (NYSE: DKS). CEO Lauren Hobart. Customer Service 1-877-846-9997. Press contacts via dickssportinggoods.com/about-us/press-releases. Marketing organisation sits under EVP/Chief Marketing Officer Emily Silver.

name: Academy Sports + Outdoors
regions: ~18 states (TX, FL, GA, AL, TN, NC, SC, MO, OK, AR, LA, MS, KY, IN, OH, WV, VA, KS)
why_target: Largest sporting goods chain in the Sun Belt with massive Texas + Florida footprint, both WC2026 host states (Dallas, Houston, Atlanta and Miami are 2026 host cities); Latino market overlap perfect for Mexico + Argentina + Brazil jersey campaigns
notes: HQ: 1800 N Mason Rd Katy TX 77449 (Houston metro). Parent: Academy Sports and Outdoors Inc (NASDAQ: ASO). CEO Steve Lawrence. Customer Service 1-888-922-2336. Note: NYSE/NASDAQ-listed so investor-grade press pitching works.

name: REI Co-op
why_target: Co-op member-owned outdoor and active-lifestyle retailer with 24M+ members; less football-heavy historically but co-op model means individual store managers have community-event latitude; family/casual demo aligns with host-nation casual-fan WC2026 surge
notes: HQ: 6750 S 228th St, Kent, WA 98032 (Seattle metro, a 2026 host city). Privately held co-op. CEO Mary Beth Laughton. Customer Service 1-800-426-4840. Each REI store has a community events programme that can be approached locally.

name: Big 5 Sporting Goods
regions: Western US (CA, AZ, NV, OR, WA, ID, UT, NM, CO, TX, OK, WY, MT)
why_target: Western US chain dominant in California, a state with the largest Mexican-American population in the US; Mexico jersey sales surge during WC2026 plays directly into Big 5's core SoCal / Central Valley / Bay Area footprint
notes: HQ: 2525 E El Segundo Blvd El Segundo CA 90245. Parent: Big 5 Sporting Goods Corporation (formerly NASDAQ: BGFV; taken private by Worldwide Sports Group in 2025). Customer Service 1-877-244-7547. CEO Steven Miller.

name: Hibbett (incl. City Gear)
why_target: Largest US sneaker chain in small-town markets across the South and Midwest; ~1170 stores including City Gear urban-format banner; strong Nike/adidas football boot inventory; underserved football catchment in rural Sun Belt that gets the host-nation lift
notes: HQ: 2700 Milan Court Birmingham AL 35211. Acquired by JD Sports Fashion plc in 2024 (LSE: JD.). Customer Service 1-844-362-1762. President Jared Briskin. City Gear focuses on urban/youth markets - perfect for Latino jersey crossover.

name: Foot Locker US
why_target: Largest US sneaker mall chain; carries WC2026 Nike/adidas/Jordan replica jerseys and lifestyle football crossover; mall foot traffic spikes during summer WC2026 schedule (June-July 2026)
notes: HQ: 330 West 34th Street, New York, NY 10001 (but relocating to St Petersburg FL 2025-26). Parent: Foot Locker Inc (NYSE: FL). Customer Service 1-800-991-6815. CEO Mary Dillon. Banners include Foot Locker, Kids Foot Locker, Champs Sports, atmos.

name: Champs Sports
best_contact_role: Customer Service / Foot Locker Inc Marketing
why_target: Foot Locker Inc's team-sport-leaning banner; carries jerseys + boots + supporters' gear with a more athletic-team positioning than Foot Locker main; WC2026 replica kits + boot launches
notes: Subsidiary of Foot Locker Inc (NYSE: FL). HQ shared with Foot Locker (NYC and St Petersburg FL). Customer Service 1-888-200-9081. Marketing decisions roll up to Foot Locker Inc EVP/CMO Kim Waldmann.

name: Finish Line

name: Soccer.com (Eurosport)
